     (see .agent_instructions/adr_frontmatter.md). Edit the ADRs, not this file. -->

# Architecture Decision Records — Index

Derived index of every ADR in `docs/adr/`, generated from each file's YAML frontmatter.
It is a regenerable cache: **do not hand-edit**. To refresh it after adding or changing an
ADR, rerun the generator documented in
[`.agent_instructions/adr_frontmatter.md`](../../.agent_instructions/adr_frontmatter.md).

Identity is the `id` (filename stem), not the leading number — numbers may repeat (see D6).

| id | Title | Status | Tags | Summary |
|----|-------|--------|------|---------|
| `0001-record-architecture-decisions` | Record architecture decisions | Accepted | meta | Brighter will use Architecture Decision Records (as described by Michael Nygard) to record the architectural decisions made on the project, enabling lightweight, version-controlled documentation of significant design choices. |
| `0002-use-a-single-threaded-message-pump` | Use A Single Threaded Message Pump | Accepted | message-pump, reactor, proactor, concurrency | Brighter uses a single-threaded message pump (Reactor pattern) to preserve message ordering and ensure sequential access to shared resources, supporting multiple pump instances for throughput and asynchronous handlers (Proactor pattern) to avoid blocking on I/O. |
| `0003-use-claim-check-for-large-messages` | Use A Claim Check For Large or Sensitive Messages | Proposed | claim-check, messaging, message-mapping | Brighter will implement the Claim Check pattern to handle large or sensitive messages by extracting the payload into an external backing store and placing a UUID-based claim check key in the message header, with Check-Luggage, Store-Luggage, and Data-Enricher components abstracting the storage. |
| `0004-use-an-envelope-wrapper-with-transports` | Use An Envelope Wrapper with Transports | Proposed | message-mapping, pipeline, middleware, transports | Brighter will implement a Wrap-Unwrap pipeline for the Message Mapper, allowing common message transformations (such as claim checks) to be mixed in via attributes on the mapper, extending existing behaviour without breaking changes to current code. |
| `0005-support-async-pipelines` | Support Async Pipelines | Accepted | async, pipeline, message-pump, concurrency | Brighter implements support for asynchronous handler and message mapper pipelines via a custom synchronization context that invokes callbacks on the message pump thread, with the requirement that pipelines be homogeneously synchronous or asynchronous. |
| `0006-blocking-and-non-blocking-retries` | Blocking and Non-Blocking Retry | Accepted | retry, resilience, dead-letter-queue, error-handling | Brighter supports both blocking retries via Polly policies (UsePolicy attribute) and non-blocking retries via DeferMessageAction for transient failures, with broker re-queuing and a dead-letter channel threshold; non-transient failures should be logged and the message acknowledged. |
| `0007-aspect-oriented-programming` | Aspect Oriented Programming | Accepted | aop, pipeline, middleware, architecture | Brighter uses Aspect Oriented Programming via .NET attributes as pointcuts and a Russian doll pipeline as advice, allowing cross-cutting concerns such as logging, retries, and exception handling to be configured directly on handlers and mappers while remaining visible in the handler code. |
| `0008-support-cloud-events` | Support Cloud Events | Accepted | cloudevents, messaging, transports, message-mapping | Brighter will support the CloudEvents specification by adding CloudEvents metadata fields to the MessageHeader and enabling transports to produce and consume both binary and structured CloudEvents, with the Publication passed to the Message Mapper to supply user-defined CloudEvents values. |
| `0009-expose-request-context` | Expose Request Context | Accepted | request-context, pipeline, messaging | Adds an optional RequestContext parameter to CommandProcessor transmission methods so callers can supply their own context instead of relying on the factory, enabling the Dispatcher to originate a shared context that flows through the translation and handler pipelines. |
| `0010-brighter-semantic-conventions` | Brighter OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions | Accepted | observability, otel, tracing, messaging | Defines Brighter's OpenTelemetry semantic conventions for span naming, attributes, and context propagation for both Command Processor operations (send, publish, deposit, clear) and Dispatcher Performer (consumer) spans. |
| `0011-brighter-support-for-bulk-messaging-operations` | Support for Bulk Messaging Operations | Proposed | bulk-messaging, outbox, messaging | Introduces a StartBatch/EndBatch mechanism on the Outbox to support bulk DepositPost, using late-binding reflection to reuse the single-request pipeline and accumulate writes to the Outbox rather than writing each message individually. |
| `0012-provide-in-memory-and-simple-implementations` | Provide In-Memory and Simple Implementations | Accepted | testing, inbox, outbox, architecture | Decides to provide officially supported in-memory and simple implementations of Brighter's core abstractions (producers, consumers, outboxes, inboxes, factories) for use in end-user testing, replacing ad-hoc test doubles with well-tested, reusable helpers. |
| `0013-publish-should-be-in-parallel` | Publish Should Be In Parallel | Accepted | publish, concurrency, async, messaging | Changes CommandProcessor.Publish to invoke subscribed handlers in parallel using Parallel.ForEach (sync) and Task.WhenAll (async), requiring RequestContext to be made thread-safe via ConcurrentDictionary for span data and bag properties. |
| `0014-di-friendly-framework` | DI Friendly Framework | Accepted | di, architecture, api-design | Adopts the DI-Friendly Framework pattern, defining per-family Factory interfaces (e.g., IAmAHandlerFactory) for each user-defined type family rather than abstracting an IoC container, keeping the IoC container an optional user choice. |
| `0015-push-button-api-for-dsl` | A Push Button API must underlie any Fluent Interface | Accepted | dsl, api-design, architecture | Decides that all Fluent (DSL) APIs in Brighter must be implemented as Fowler's Expression Builder over an underlying command-query (push-button) API, ensuring the underlying API can be used and tested independently of the fluent layer. |
| `0016-use-configuration-as-code-not-by-convention` | Use Configuration As Code, Not Configuration By Convention | Accepted | configuration, architecture, api-design | Establishes that Brighter uses configuration-as-code rather than convention-over-configuration, requiring explicit interface and generic declarations rather than naming conventions or implicit defaults, optimizing for code ownership over initial authorship. |
| `0017-use-strict-cqs-across-brighter-and-darker` | Use Strict Command Query Separation Across Brighter and Darker | Accepted | cqrs, architecture, api-design | Brighter treats all IRequest derivatives (Command, Event) as Commands in CQS terms — they mutate state and return no value; queries belong to Darker, enabling a full CQRS architecture when the two libraries are used together. |
| `0018-support-a-command-oriented-interface` | Support A Command Oriented Interface | Accepted | cqrs, architecture, api-design | Brighter is designated as the Command side of CQRS, supporting Martin Fowler's Command Oriented Interface pattern by handling all command operations while Darker handles queries. |
| `0019-avoid-primitive-obsession` | Avoid Primitive Obsession | Accepted | api-design, architecture | Public and internal interfaces must use domain-typed value objects (e.g. RoutingKey, ChannelName) rather than bare primitives; record structs are preferred for new value objects to reduce allocation cost and eliminate parameter-ordering errors. |
| `0020-reduce-esb-complexity` | Reduce External Service Bus Complexity | Deprecated | architecture, messaging, outbox, mediator | Attempted to refactor ExternalServiceBus by extracting a MessagePoster and Outbox class, but the approach was abandoned because the extracted classes developed cyclic dependencies, confirming that the Mediator role of ExternalServiceBus is necessary. |
| `0021-move-archive-from-esb` | Move Archive Methods from External Service Bus to Outbox Archiver to Reduce Complexity | Accepted | archive, outbox, messaging, architecture | Moves the Archive and ArchiveAsync methods from ExternalServiceBus into OutboxArchiver, and renames ExternalServiceBus to OutboxProducerMediator to better reflect its mediator role between producer and outbox. |
| `0022-generate-metrics-from-traces` | Generate Metrics from Traces | Proposed | observability, otel, metrics, tracing | Brighter will implement a custom OpenTelemetry processor (BrighterMetricsFromTracesProcessor) that derives standard messaging and database metrics from existing trace spans, registered automatically via AddBrighterInstrumentation when the Brighter meter is enabled. |
| `0023-reactor-and-nonblocking-io` | Reactor and Nonblocking IO, Proactor and Blocking IO | Accepted | reactor, async, message-pump, concurrency | Brighter explicitly supports both the Reactor (blocking I/O, single-threaded pump) and Proactor (non-blocking I/O, yields between Performers) concurrency models across all transports, renames message pump classes to Reactor/Proactor, and adopts an internal fork of AsyncEx's AsyncContext as BrighterSynchronizationContext to run continuations on the message pump thread. |
| `0024-scheduling-requests-and-messages` | Scheduling Requests and Messages | Proposed | scheduling, messaging, aws, api-design | Adds scheduling support to Brighter via new IAmAMessageScheduler and IAmARequestScheduler interfaces, extending IAmACommandProcessor with delay overloads and providing pluggable scheduler implementations including in-memory, Hangfire, Quartz, AWS Scheduler, and Azure Service Bus. |
| `0025-sqs-support-for-cloudevents` | SQS support for CloudEvents | Accepted | cloudevents, sqs, aws, transports | Decides to add CloudEvents support to the SQS transport; note: the decision body appears to contain incomplete placeholder content. |
| `0026-use-source-generated-logging` | Use Source Generated Logging | Accepted | logging, observability, performance | Adopts source-generated logging (LoggerMessage attribute) throughout the Brighter src folder, replacing string-templated logging for improved performance and type safety, with a direct 1-to-1 migration approach assisted by LLMs. |
| `0027-allow-custom-publication-finder` | Allow custom publication finder | Accepted | publish, messaging, configuration, api-design | Introduces IAmAPublicationFinder to locate the appropriate Publication from a producer registry and request context, with a default implementation that resolves by RequestContext.Topic, PublicationTopicAttribute, or request type, enabling dynamic multi-topic routing. |
| `0028-support-circuit-breaking-of-topics` | Support Circuit Breaking for Topics in Outbox Sweeper | Proposed | circuit-breaker, outbox, resilience, messaging | Adds a circuit-breaker mechanism to the outbox sweeper that tracks per-topic dispatch failures and temporarily skips tripped topics for a configurable cooldown period, preventing a failing topic from blocking dispatch of healthy topics. |
| `0029-cloudeventstype-message-pump` | Use CloudEventsType for Message Pump | Accepted | message-pump, cloudevents, messaging, reactive | Replaces compile-time generic type parameters on Reactor and Proactor with a runtime Func<Message, Type> strategy so that a single subscription channel can receive and route multiple CloudEvents message types based on message metadata. |
| `0030-add-a-mediator` | Add a Mediator to Brighter | Proposed | mediator, workflow, architecture, messaging | Adds a Mediator class that orchestrates multi-step workflows (sequence, choice, parallel, wait) over CommandProcessor-dispatched handlers, providing a first-class orchestration alternative to emergent choreography in Brighter. |
| `0031-support-agreement-dispatcher` | Support Agreement Dispatcher | Proposed | dispatcher, messaging, architecture, workflow | Changes SubscriberRegistry to accept a lambda routing strategy (IRequest x IRequestContext -> List<Type>) enabling dynamic handler selection based on message content or context, following Fowler's Agreement Dispatcher pattern. |
| `0032-remove-explicit-clear-lock` | Remove Semaphore from Explicit Clear | Accepted | outbox, concurrency, locking, resilience | Removes the blocking _clearSemaphoreToken from OutboxProducerMediator's explicit clear path, relying on IDistributedLock for dual-publish protection instead, to prevent HTTP requests from serialising behind sequential semaphore waits at scale. |
| `0033-lifetime-of-command-processor-and-mediator` | Lifetime of Command Processor and Mediator | Proposed | lifetime, di, mediator, outbox | Removes the static IAmABoxTransactionProvider from CommandProcessor, scoping the transaction provider to handler instances instead, and reverts CommandProcessor to a singleton lifetime. |
| `0034-optimise-reads-and-writes-from-dynamo-outbox` | Optimise reads/writes from/to Dynamo outbox | Proposed | outbox, dynamo, performance, aws | Optimises DynamoDB outbox performance by introducing new all-topics GSIs for parallel scanning, adding sharding to the Delivered index, using bulk get/write operations, and replacing full outstanding-message loads with a count-only scan. |
| `0035-generated-test` | Test Generation Tool | Proposed | testing, test-generation | Introduces a command-line test generation tool (Paramore.Brighter.Test.Generator) using Liquid templates and a test-configuration.json file to produce consistent, provider-specific test suites across all outbox and gateway implementations. |
| `0036-memory-leak-tests` | Memory Leak Testing Infrastructure | Proposed | testing, memory, performance | Establishes a two-tier memory leak testing infrastructure using JetBrains dotMemory Unit and WebApplicationFactory: quick tests (5-10 minutes) run on every PR to catch obvious leaks, and soak tests (30-60 minutes) run nightly to detect gradual accumulation. |
| `0037-add-messaging-gateway-generated-test` | Add Messaging Gateway Generated Tests | Accepted | testing, test-generation, messaging | Extends the test generator tool to support messaging gateway tests via Liquid templates and a MessagingGatewayConfiguration, enabling consistent test coverage across RabbitMQ, AWS SNS/SQS, Azure Service Bus, and other gateway implementations with minimal per-gateway code. |
| `0037-aws-test-resource-cleanup` | AWS Test Resource Cleanup | Accepted | testing, aws, sqs, sns | Adopts a tag-based cleanup strategy for orphaned AWS test resources by making SnsAttributes.Tags configurable, tagging all test SQS queues and SNS topics with Environment=Test, and replacing the indiscriminate cleanup script with one that filters by tag via the AWS Resource Groups Tagging API. |
| `0037-provide-roslyn-analyzers-for-brighter` | Provide Roslyn Analyzers for Brighter | Proposed | roslyn-analyzers, api-design, meta | Introduces a Paramore.Brighter.Analyzer NuGet package with Roslyn analyzers for compile-time validation of Brighter usage patterns, initially covering Publication.RequestType assignment, Subscription constructor arguments, and Wrap attribute placement. |
| `0037-reject-message-on-error-handler` | Reject Message On Error Handler | Accepted | message-rejection, dead-letter-queue, error-handling, middleware | Introduces RejectMessageOnErrorAttribute and RejectMessageOnErrorHandler — a declarative middleware pair that catches all unhandled exceptions in message handlers, logs them, and converts them to RejectMessageAction for automatic dead-letter-queue routing. |
| `0037-universal-scheduler-delay` | Universal Scheduler Delay Support | Accepted | scheduling, messaging, transports, message-pump | Decides to make delayed message delivery consistent across all Brighter transports by routing delayed requeues through the IAmAMessageScheduler interface rather than direct timer calls, with InMemoryScheduler as the default and lazy producer creation in consumers for transports without native delay support. |
| `0038-aws-sqs-dlq-direct-send` | AWS SQS DLQ: Replace ChangeMessageVisibility with Direct Send | Accepted | aws, sqs, dead-letter-queue, transports | Decides to replace SQS's ChangeMessageVisibility(0) rejection path with direct send to a Brighter-managed DLQ queue followed by immediate deletion from the source queue, following the Kafka pattern established in ADR 0034 and rejection routing strategy from ADR 0036. |
| `0038-dont-ack-action` | Don't Ack Action | Accepted | message-pump, message-rejection, error-handling, messaging | Adds a DontAckAction exception type that signals the message pump to nack (rather than acknowledge) a message on queue-based transports, plus DontAckOnErrorAttribute and a FeatureSwitchAttribute.DontAck option for blocking on feature-disabled or error scenarios. |
| `0038-remove-clear-service-bus` | Remove ClearServiceBus | Accepted | architecture, mediator, testing | Decides to empty CommandProcessor.ClearServiceBus() to a no-op and remove all internal call sites and [Collection(\"CommandProcessor\")] xUnit attributes, since ADR 0034's instance-based mediator eliminated the static state that originally required this workaround. |
| `0039-opentelemetry-builder-extension` | OpenTelemetry Builder Extension | Proposed | observability, otel, tracing, metrics | Adds a new Paramore.Brighter.Extensions.OpenTelemetry package providing a single AddBrighterInstrumentation() extension on OpenTelemetryBuilder that wires both tracing and metrics in one call, delegating to the existing per-signal extensions in Extensions.Diagnostics. |
| `0039-redis-dlq-brighter-managed` | Redis Dead Letter Queue — Brighter-Managed | Accepted | redis, dead-letter-queue, transports, error-handling | Adds Brighter-managed DLQ support to the Redis transport by using lazy producer creation to send rejected messages to a configured DLQ or invalid message channel instead of silently discarding them, following the rejection routing strategy from ADR 0036. |
| `0039-scoping-dependencies-inline-with-lifetime-scope` | Scoping dependencies inline with lifetime scope | Proposed | di, lifetime, pipeline, publish | Decides to create a new lifetime scope per registered subscriber when invoking Publish rather than wrapping the entire CommandProcessor in a single scope, eliminating the need for a CommandProcessorProvider and ensuring handlers don't unexpectedly share scoped dependencies. |
| `0039-transport-scheduler-wiring` | Transport Channel Factory Scheduler Wiring | Accepted | scheduling, transports, di, configuration | Decides to thread IAmAMessageScheduler through the channel factory chain via constructor injection and a new IAmAChannelFactoryWithScheduler opt-in interface, register InMemorySchedulerFactory as the default scheduler, and create missing MQTT factory infrastructure. |
| `0040-add-the-specification-pattern` | Add the Specification Pattern | Proposed | specification-pattern, mediator, architecture | Adds a custom implementation of the Specification Pattern to Brighter for use in mediator workflow branching conditions and the Agreement Dispatcher, inspired by Huddle Engineering's implementation but simplified with modern C# lambda expressions. |
| `0040-mssql-dlq-brighter-managed` | MsSql Dead Letter Queue — Brighter-Managed | Accepted | dead-letter-queue, mssql, messaging, error-handling | Adds Brighter-managed DLQ support to the MsSql transport by implementing Reject()/RejectAsync() to forward rejected messages to a configurable dead letter topic using a lazy MsSqlMessageProducer, following the pattern established by Kafka, SQS, and Redis. |
| `0041-add-parallel-split-to-mediator` | Implementing Parallel Split Step for Concurrent Workflow Execution | Proposed | mediator, workflow, concurrency, async | Introduces ParallelSplitStep<TData> and a producer-consumer model (Scheduler + Runner) using bounded in-memory channels to enable concurrent branch execution in the mediator workflow, deferring resilient out-of-process storage to a future ADR. |
| `0041-postgres-dlq-brighter-managed` | PostgreSQL Dead Letter Queue — Brighter-Managed | Accepted | dead-letter-queue, postgres, messaging, error-handling | Adds Brighter-managed DLQ support to the PostgreSQL transport using a two-step reject flow (forward to DLQ then delete the source message from the queue table) via lazily-created PostgresMessageProducer instances, consistent with the established cross-transport DLQ pattern. |
| `0042-rocketmq-dlq-brighter-managed` | RocketMQ Dead Letter Queue — Brighter-Managed | Accepted | dead-letter-queue, rocketmq, messaging, error-handling | Adds Brighter-managed DLQ support to the RocketMQ transport, replacing the infinite requeue loop in Reject() with async lazy DLQ producer creation and Ack()-based source message cleanup on the broker. |
| `0042-use-reactive-programming-for-mediator` | Use Reactive Programming For Mediator | Accepted | mediator, workflow, reactive, concurrency | Adopts Flow Based Programming (FBP) for the mediator where each Step derives from a Component with a typed In port (IAmAJobChannel), enabling parallel splits and merges through multiple Out or In ports and bracket-based synchronization. |
| `0043-mqtt-dlq-brighter-managed` | MQTT Dead Letter Queue — Brighter-Managed | Accepted | dead-letter-queue, mqtt, messaging, error-handling | Adds Brighter-managed DLQ support to the MQTT transport using Lazy<T> producers following the Redis pattern, and creates the missing MqttSubscription and MqttMessageConsumerFactory classes required for standard DLQ configuration. |
| `0043-rabbitmq-mutual-tls` | Optional Mutual TLS (mTLS) Support for RabbitMQ Messaging Gateway | Proposed | rabbitmq, security, tls, transports | Introduces opt-in mutual TLS (mTLS) support for RabbitMQ messaging gateways, allowing X.509 client certificate configuration while preserving backwards compatibility for non-TLS and TLS-only deployments. |
| `0044-instance-based-mediator-for-testability` | Instance-Based Mediator for Testability | Proposed | mediator, di, testing, architecture | Converts the static OutboxProducerMediator singleton to an instance field in CommandProcessor, enforcing singleton semantics via DI registration to enable parallel test execution with isolated mediator instances per test. |
| `0045-provide-dlq-where-missing` | Provide a Dead Letter Channel Where Native Support is Missing | Accepted | dead-letter-queue, messaging, error-handling, transports | Adds a Brighter-managed dead letter channel for transports lacking native DLQ support via a new RejectMessageAction exception and IAmAMessageProducer direct publishing, and an InvalidMessageAction for deserialization failures routed to a configurable invalid message channel. |
| `0046-kafka-dlq-producer-for-requeue` | Kafka DLQ Producer for Requeue | Accepted | kafka, dead-letter-queue, transports, messaging | Defines how KafkaMessageConsumer creates and owns lazy-initialized KafkaMessageProducer instances for dead letter and invalid message channels, inheriting consumer configuration and matching the data topic's MakeChannels strategy for topic creation. |
| `0047-message-rejection-routing-strategy` | Message Rejection Routing Strategy | Accepted | message-rejection, dead-letter-queue, error-handling, messaging | Defines the Reject() routing logic that directs DeliveryError messages to the dead letter queue and Unacceptable messages to the invalid message channel (with DLQ fallback), enriching rejected messages with origin metadata and acknowledging after attempted channel production. |
| `0048-support-native-json-payload-storage-for-rdbms-inbox` | Support Native JSON Payload Storage for RDBMS Inbox | Proposed | inbox, serialization, postgres | Extends the RDBMS Inbox to optionally store message payloads in native JSON column types (PostgreSQL JSON/JSONB, MySQL JSON, Spanner JSON) via a new JsonMessagePayload flag on IAmARelationalDatabaseConfiguration, preserving existing default behaviour. |
| `0049-testing-assembly-structure` | Testing Assembly Structure | Accepted | testing, api-design, architecture | Establishes a new Paramore.Brighter.Testing NuGet package containing SpyCommandProcessor, CommandType enum, and RecordedCall record as the official, maintained testing utilities for verifying handler interactions with IAmACommandProcessor. |
| `0050-spy-command-processor-api` | Spy Command Processor API | Proposed | testing, api-design, mediator | Designs a three-layer SpyCommandProcessor public API — quick checks (WasCalled, CallCount, Observe<T>), request inspection (GetRequests<T>, GetCalls), and full detail access (RecordedCalls, DepositedRequests) — with all interface methods virtual to support subclassing. |
| `0051-error-handling-examples` | Error Handling Example Applications | Accepted | error-handling, kafka, rabbitmq, messaging | Defines six sample TaskQueue applications demonstrating RejectOnError, DeferOnError, and DontAckOnError error-handling actions for Kafka and RabbitMQ, each following the established Brighter sample convention with Greetings, GreetingsSender, GreetingsReceiverConsole, and optional DlqConsole sub-projects. |
| `0051-expose-unacceptable-message-limit-window` | Expose UnacceptableMessageLimitWindow Through Configuration Pipeline | Accepted | configuration, messaging, message-pump, dead-letter-queue | Wires the missing `UnacceptableMessageLimitWindow` property through `ConsumerFactory` and all 13 transport-specific subscription constructors, following the identical pattern used by `UnacceptableMessageLimit` so that users can configure the window on any subscription type and have it take effect at runtime. |
| `0052-defer-message-on-error-backstop-handler` | Defer Message On Error Backstop Handler | Accepted | pipeline, middleware, error-handling, resilience | Adds `DeferMessageOnErrorAttribute` and handler pair (sync and async) that wraps the pipeline in a try/catch and throws `DeferMessageAction` with a configurable per-handler delay, completing the set of three declarative backstop attributes and extending the message pump to read the delay from the exception. |
| `0053-box-database-migration` | Box Database Migration | Accepted | outbox, inbox, migration, provisioning | Introduces a modular `Paramore.Brighter.BoxProvisioning` library with database-backend-specific packages that create and migrate Inbox and Outbox tables at startup via `IAmABoxProvisioner`, `IAmABoxMigrationRunner`, and a `BoxProvisioningHostedService`, replacing ad-hoc sample-only schema creation code. |
| `0053-fix-resilience-pipeline-reuse` | Fix Resilience Pipeline Reuse Across Command Handlers | Accepted | pipeline, resilience, middleware | Fixes `ResilienceExceptionPolicyHandlerAsync<TRequest>` which ignored the `UseTypePipeline` flag and always called `GetPipeline<TRequest>()`, preventing non-generic shared `ResiliencePipeline` instances from being reused across multiple command handler types. |
| `0053-pipeline-validation-at-startup` | Pipeline Validation and Diagnostic Report at Startup | Accepted | pipeline, configuration, specification-pattern, observability | Introduces opt-in startup pipeline validation and diagnostic reporting via `IAmAPipelineValidator` and `IAmAPipelineDiagnosticWriter`, using a Specification pattern to check handler pipeline ordering, producer publications, and consumer subscriptions across all three Brighter configuration paths. |
| `0054-rmq-shutdown-and-connection-pool-fixes` | Fix RMQ Shutdown Deadlock and Connection Pool Race Condition | Accepted | rabbitmq, transports, async, concurrency | Fixes a sync-over-async shutdown deadlock in the RabbitMQ Proactor by adding `IAsyncDisposable` to `IAmAChannelAsync` and awaiting `DisposeAsync()` on MT_QUIT, and resolves a connection pool race condition where stale `ConnectionShutdown` handlers could dispose active connections via `ReferenceEquals` guards and `ConfigureAwait(false)`. |
| `0054-roslyn-analyzer-extensions-for-pipeline-validation` | Roslyn Analyzer Extensions for Pipeline Validation | Proposed | roslyn-analyzers, pipeline, meta | Adds three Roslyn analyzer diagnostics (BRT006–BRT008) to `Paramore.Brighter.Analyzer` to detect at compile time: backstop attributes ordered after resilience pipelines, sync/async attribute mismatches on handlers, and subscription pump type mismatches with handler types. |
| `0055-kafka-nack-seek-to-offset` | Kafka Nack Seeks to Offset for Redelivery | Accepted | kafka, transports, messaging, error-handling | Fixes `KafkaMessageConsumer.Nack` from a no-op to calling `_consumer.Seek(topicPartitionOffset)` to rewind the consumer position to the nacked message's offset, fulfilling the `IAmAMessageConsumerSync.Nack` contract that nacked messages are genuinely redelivered. |
| `0056-timed-outbox-archiver-sync-fallback` | TimedOutboxArchiver Sync Fallback | Accepted | outbox, archive, async | Adds `HasAsyncOutbox()` and `HasOutbox()` methods to `OutboxArchiver` following the naming convention of `OutboxProducerMediator`, then updates `TimedOutboxArchiver.Archive` to prefer the async path, fall back to sync via `Task.Run`, and log a warning when neither outbox is configured. This fixes issue #3670 where `TimedOutboxArchiver` unconditionally called `ArchiveAsync` and threw `ArgumentException` when only a sync outbox was registered. |
| `0057-box-schema-versioning-and-migrations` | Box Schema Versioning and Migrations | Accepted | outbox, inbox, migration, provisioning | Replaces ADR 0053's single-version provisioning model with a versioned migration chain (7 outbox versions, up to 2 inbox versions per backend) and a three-path provisioner (fresh install / bootstrap of existing unversioned tables / normal incremental upgrade) for all relational backends. Introduces discriminator-gated version detection, per-backend idempotent ALTER patterns, extends `IAmABoxMigration` with `LogicalColumns` for detection, and adds `IAmABoxMigrationCatalog` to separate the historical V1 DDL from the current live-builder DDL. |
| `0057-in-memory-box-abstract-expiry` | Make InMemoryBox Abstract with Subclass-Specific Expiry Strategies | Accepted | outbox, inbox, memory, configuration | Makes `InMemoryBox<T>` abstract with subclass-defined `RemoveExpiredMessages` and `Compact` hooks so `InMemoryOutbox` only evicts dispatched messages (preventing silent loss of undispatched messages) while `InMemoryInbox` retains its existing write-time TTL eviction. Adds an `EntryLimit = -1` option to disable compaction and exposes default outbox configuration via a new `InMemoryBoxConfiguration` record on `ProducersConfiguration`. |
| `0057-span-based-performance` | Reduce Heap Allocations with Span<T> and Memory<T> | Accepted | performance, memory, messaging, transports | Adopts `Span<T>`, `ReadOnlySpan<T>`, `Memory<T>`, and `ReadOnlyMemory<byte>` across Brighter's core message pipeline and transport gateways to eliminate redundant heap allocations, replacing `MessageBody`'s internal `byte[]` storage with zero-copy `ReadOnlyMemory<byte>`, enabling direct UTF-8 serialization in `JsonMessageMapper`, and reducing per-header encoding allocations in the RMQ, Kafka, and Azure Service Bus transports. Also fixes the Azure Service Bus `Encoding.Default` bug and the Kafka ASCII/UTF-8 encoding mismatch. |
| `0058-box-provisioning-rdd-role-interfaces` | Box Provisioning RDD Role Interfaces and Template-Method Runner | Accepted | box, provisioning, architecture, migration | Introduces role-based instance interfaces (`IAmABoxMigrationDetectionHelper`, `IAmABoxMigrationCatalog`, `IAmABoxPayloadModeValidator`, `IAmAProvisioningUnitOfWork`) to unify the BoxProvisioning family under Responsibility-Driven Design naming conventions, converting static helper and factory classes to instance classes. Adds `SqlBoxMigrationRunner<TConnection, TTransaction>` abstract base that seals the three-path migration algorithm (fresh/bootstrap/normal) with a harmonized unit-of-work lifecycle encoding each backend's specific lock/transaction ordering and cancellation contract. |
| `0059-box-provisioning-abstract-base-naming-symmetry` | Naming symmetry for BoxProvisioning abstract bases — rename `RelationalBoxMigrationRunnerBase` → `SqlBoxMigrationRunner` | Accepted | box, provisioning, architecture, meta | Renames `RelationalBoxMigrationRunnerBase<TConnection, TTransaction>` to `SqlBoxMigrationRunner<TConnection, TTransaction>` so it is symmetric with the sibling `SqlBoxProvisioner` abstract base introduced in ADR 0058 §B.5, applying the `Sql*` prefix (which precisely names the `DbConnection` ADO.NET constraint) and dropping the `*Base` suffix. This is a pure Tidy First rename with no behavioral change, closing the documented naming asymmetry before PR #4039 merges so no source break is incurred by adopters. |
| `0060-multi-tenancy-migration-history-scope` | Multi-Tenancy: Per-Schema Placement of the Box Migration-History Table | Accepted | box, migration, multi-tenancy, provisioning | Introduces an opt-in `MigrationHistoryScope.PerSchema` enum on `BoxProvisioningOptions` and a single `ResolveHistorySchema()` method on `SqlBoxMigrationRunner` so MSSQL and PostgreSQL tenants can co-locate the `__BrighterMigrationHistory` table in their own schema rather than the shared default schema, with a backward-compatible `Global = 0` default. Includes a misconfiguration guard, an idempotent one-time seed that copies prior history rows from the legacy default-schema table on first per-schema creation, and schema-aware coordination of write and read sides through the detection helper. |
| `0061-box-provisioning-value-types` | Box Provisioning value types | Accepted | box, provisioning, api-design, architecture | Introduces six `record` value types (`BoxTableName`, `SchemaName`, `MigrationVersion`, `MigrationDescription`, `SqlScript`, `SourceReference`) in `Paramore.Brighter.BoxProvisioning` to replace bare string/int primitives in `IAmABoxMigration`, `IAmABoxMigrationRunner.MigrateAsync`, and `IAmABoxProvisioner.BoxTableName`, giving compile-time parameter distinctness so transposed adjacent strings produce a type error rather than a silent runtime bug. Bidirectional implicit conversions preserve source compatibility at all existing call sites with no argument changes required. |
| `0061-reject_mapping_errors` | Route Mapping Failures Through RejectMessage | Accepted | messaging, message-pump, error-handling, message-rejection | Routes MessageMappingException failures in the Proactor and Reactor message pumps through the existing RejectMessage path with RejectionReason.Unacceptable, so malformed messages are directed to the configured IMQ/DLQ instead of being silently deleted via fall-through acknowledge. |
| `0062-pg-advisory-lock-sha256` | PostgreSQL Box-Provisioning Advisory Lock — SHA-256 64-bit Key Derivation | Accepted | locking, postgres, provisioning, migration | Switches PostgreSqlAdvisoryLock from PostgreSQL's two-int4 hashtext-based advisory-lock overloads to single-argument bigint overloads, deriving a 64-bit key in C# via SHA-256 over a namespace-prefixed lockKey string, reducing the birthday-bound collision probability from approximately 1-in-2^32 to 1-in-2^64. |
| `0063-failed-delivery-context` | Failed Delivery Context for Confirmation-Based Producers | Accepted | publish, observability, resilience, error-handling | Enriches the ISupportPublishConfirmation.OnMessagePublished event to carry a PublishConfirmationResult (wire topic, original publish ActivityContext, message id) so OutboxProducerMediator can emit a linked confirmation span, log a warning, and trip the circuit breaker on async publish failures from Kafka and RMQ producers. |
| `0063-request-validation-handler` | Request validation handler | Proposed | pipeline, request-validation, middleware, di | Adds opt-in request validation to Brighter's handler pipeline via a ValidateRequest attribute and abstract ValidateRequestHandler<TRequest> in the core assembly, with separate provider packages for FluentValidation, DataAnnotations, and Brighter's Specification pattern; validation failures throw a unified RequestValidationException. |
| `0064-pipeline-cache-type-key` | Key pipeline-builder metadata caches by runtime Type | Accepted | pipeline, middleware, performance | Fixes a pipeline-cache collision bug in PipelineBuilder, TransformPipelineBuilder, and TransformPipelineBuilderAsync by replacing the simple class-name string cache key with the runtime System.Type, so same-named handler or mapper types in different namespaces each receive their own correct decorators and transforms. |
| `0064-validate-pipeline-assembly-and-provider-registration` | Validate Pipeline Assembly Scanning and Validation-Provider Registration | Accepted | pipeline, configuration, request-validation, di | Extends ValidatePipelines() with two new ISpecification<T> rule families that emit startup Warning findings when a declared transform's transformer type is not registered in the DI container, or when a handler marked with ValidateRequest has no validation provider wired up. |
| `0065-add-frontmatter-to-adrs` | Add Frontmatter to ADRs | Proposed | meta, architecture | Adds YAML frontmatter (id, title, status, author, created, summary, tags) to every ADR so agents can find prior art cheaply, and adopts the filename slug as the stable identity so duplicate ADR numbers are harmless. |

_87 ADRs indexed._
